rulings.Case Title: JENNY SILVA-ROLAND ET AL VS. BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW YORK , AS TRUSTEE FOR THE CERTIFICATE HOLDERS OF CWHEQ REVOLVING HOME EQUITY LOAN TRUST, SERIES 2007-A, SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each cause of action as to each defendant. All allegations made on "information and belief" must be supported by factual allegations that form the basis of the "information and belief." Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted as to the existence and the legal effect of the documents. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. ASIAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ASIAN, INC., LAMAR HEYSTEK, FRANK FUNG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and establish that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law based on its interpretation of the Agreement (and amendments thereto). Moving party's entire argument as to both causes of action relies on its interpretation of an unambiguous contract. Moving party failed to show that their interpretation in correct as a matter of law. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. ASIAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 5, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, DOING BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AS BAYSIDE 441 LASSEN, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Summary Judgment/Adjudication as to Asian's complaint is denied without prejudice. It is moot in light of the ruling on Asian's motion for leave to file an amended complaint. Summary Adjudication on Bayside's complaint is DENIED for failure to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Summary Judgment on Bayside's complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish every element of the cause of action for breach of contract. Moving party quotes a contractual provision, but fails to show how Asian's "action" fits into the requirements/obligations of that provision and what the damages are. It also appears that the moving party is attempting to assert a cause of action based on breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. That is not the cause of action that is asserted in the complaint as the first cause of action for which the moving party seeks summary judgment/adjudication. It is also unclear what the damages are for breach of this provision as the Separate Statement presents no evidence of any non-speculative damages that may be reduced to judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
